name: labor_policy_municipal_bond_covenant_kestrel_jus_mer
premise: A Labor Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving labor policy municipal bond covenant kestrel jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Zara Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Zara Ishikawa, accountable for a defensible call in Labor Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Camila Dubois
agents.identity: I am Camila Dubois, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Labor Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Zara Ishikawa must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Zara Ishikawa do?
